title: albizia amara subsp. amara
intro: Albizia amara subsp. amara, also known as the Indian siris, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Albizia amara subsp. amara include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and support cardiovascular health. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as tinctures and dried extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
